shadow-of-moil.name: Shadow of Moil

components.materialDescription: an undead eyeball encased in a gem worth at least 150 gp

effects.description: Whenever a creature within 10 feet of the caster hits the caster with an attack, the shadows lash out and deal 2d8 Necrotic damage to that creature.
Until the spell ends, the caster has Resistance to Radiant damage.
Flame-like shadows make the caster Heavily Obscured to others and change light within 10 feet: Dim Light becomes Darkness, and Bright Light becomes Dim Light.

shadow-of-moil.description: Flame-like shadows wreathe your body until the spell ends, causing you to become heavily obscured to others. The shadows turn dim light within 10 feet of you into darkness, and bright light in the same area to dim light. Until the spell ends, you have resistance to radiant damage. In addition, whenever a creature within 10 feet of you hits you with an attack, the shadows lash out at that creature, dealing it 2d8 necrotic damage.

shape-water.name: Shape Water

options.label: Move Or Flow
options.summary: Move or change the flow of water up to 5 feet in any direction without causing damage.

options.label: Shape And Animate
options.summary: Form water into simple shapes and animate it at your direction for 1 hour.

options.label: Color Or Opacity
options.summary: Change the water's color or opacity in the same way throughout for 1 hour.

options.label: Freeze
options.summary: Freeze the water if no creatures are in it; it unfreezes in 1 hour.

modeChoice.notes: No more than two non-instantaneous Shape Water effects can be active at once, and the caster can dismiss one as an action.

effects.description: Manipulate water in a 5-foot cube (move, animate, color, or freeze)

controlOptions.name: Move Or Flow
controlOptions.effect: Move or change the flow of water up to 5 feet in any direction.
controlOptions.details: This movement doesn't have enough force to cause damage.

controlOptions.name: Shape And Animate
controlOptions.effect: Form water into simple shapes and animate it at your direction.
controlOptions.details: This change lasts for 1 hour.

controlOptions.name: Color Or Opacity
controlOptions.effect: Change the water's color or opacity in the same way throughout.
controlOptions.details: This change lasts for 1 hour.

controlOptions.name: Freeze
controlOptions.effect: Freeze the water, provided no creatures are in it.
controlOptions.details: The water unfreezes in 1 hour.

shape-water.description: You choose an area of water that you can see within range and that fits within a 5-foot cube. You manipulate it in one of the following ways: You instantaneously move or otherwise change the flow of the water as you direct, up to 5 feet in any direction. This movement doesn't have enough force to cause damage. You cause the water to form into simple shapes and animate at your direction. This change lasts for 1 hour. You change the water's color or opacity. The water must be changed in the same way throughout. This change lasts for 1 hour. You freeze the water, provided that there are no creatures in it. The water unfreezes in 1 hour. If you cast this spell multiple times, you can have no more than two of its non-instantaneous effects active at a time, and you can dismiss such an effect as an action.

shapechange.name: Shapechange

effects.description: You shape-shift into another creature for the duration or until you take a Magic action to shape-shift into a different eligible form. The new form must be of a creature that has a Challenge Rating no higher than your level or Challenge Rating. You must have seen the sort of creature before, and it can't be a Construct or an Undead. When you cast the spell, you gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to the Hit Points of the first form into which you shape-shift. These Temporary Hit Points vanish if any remain when the spell ends. Your game statistics are replaced by the stat block of the chosen form, but you retain your creature type; alignment; personality; Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores; Hit Points; Hit Point Dice; proficiencies; and ability to communicate. If you have the Spellcasting feature, you retain it too. Upon shape-shifting, you determine whether your equipment drops to the ground or changes in size and shape to fit the new form while you're in it.

grantedActions.action: Take a Magic action to shape-shift into a different eligible form.
grantedActions.notes: The current granted-action schema cannot name the Magic action subtype separately from a normal action.

controlOptions.name: Eligible form
controlOptions.effect: The caster shape-shifts into a seen non-Construct, non-Undead creature with Challenge Rating no higher than the caster level or Challenge Rating.

controlOptions.name: Repeat shape-shift
controlOptions.effect: For the duration, the caster can take a Magic action to shape-shift into a different eligible form.

controlOptions.name: Temporary hit points
controlOptions.effect: On the initial shape-shift, the caster gains temporary hit points equal to the first form Hit Points; remaining temporary hit points vanish when the spell ends.

controlOptions.name: Stat replacement
controlOptions.effect: The chosen form stat block replaces game statistics, while creature type, alignment, personality, Intelligence, Wisdom, Charisma, Hit Points, Hit Point Dice, proficiencies, communication, and Spellcasting are retained.

controlOptions.name: Equipment choice
controlOptions.effect: When shape-shifting, the caster chooses whether equipment drops or changes size and shape to fit the new form.

effects.description: On the first shape-shift, the caster gains temporary hit points equal to the Hit Points of the chosen first form.

aiContext.prompt: Ask the caster to choose an eligible seen form that is not a Construct or Undead, confirm Challenge Rating eligibility, and choose whether equipment drops or changes to fit.

shapechange.description: You shape-shift into another creature for the duration or until you take a Magic action to shape-shift into a different eligible form. The new form must be of a creature that has a Challenge Rating no higher than your level or Challenge Rating. You must have seen the sort of creature before, and it can't be a Construct or an Undead. When you cast the spell, you gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to the Hit Points of the first form into which you shape-shift. These Temporary Hit Points vanish if any remain when the spell ends. Your game statistics are replaced by the stat block of the chosen form, but you retain your creature type; alignment; personality; Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores; Hit Points; Hit Point Dice; proficiencies; and ability to communicate. If you have the Spellcasting feature, you retain it too. Upon shape-shifting, you determine whether your equipment drops to the ground or changes in size and shape to fit the new form while you're in it.

shatter.name: Shatter

saveModifiers.reason: Constructs have disadvantage on the saving throw.

shatter.description: A loud noise erupts from a point of your choice within range. Each creature in a 10-foot-radius Sphere centered there makes a Constitution saving throw, taking 3d8 Thunder damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. A Construct has Disadvantage on the save. A nonmagical object that isn't being worn or carried also takes the damage if it's in the spell's area.
shatter.higherLevels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 2.

shield.name: Shield

castingTime.reactionCondition: when you are hit by an attack or targeted by Magic Missile

combatCost.condition: when you are hit by an attack or targeted by the Magic Missile spell

effects.description: Gain +5 bonus to AC until the start of your next turn.
Take no damage from Magic Missile.

shield.description: An imperceptible barrier of magical force protects you. Until the start of your next turn, you have a +5 bonus to AC, including against the triggering attack, and you take no damage from Magic Missile .

shield-of-faith.name: Shield of Faith

effects.description: Target gains a +2 bonus to AC.

shield-of-faith.description: A shimmering field surrounds a creature of your choice within range, granting it a +2 bonus to AC for the duration.

shillelagh.name: Shillelagh

effects.description: Imbues club/quarterstaff: use spellcasting ability for attack/damage, damage die becomes d8 (scales), option for Force damage.

weaponRequirement.notes: The affected club or quarterstaff is the object held by the caster when Shillelagh is cast.

grantedAttack.notes: The spell does not create a new attack action; it modifies melee attacks made with the imbued weapon while the spell lasts.

scaling.customFormula: d8 at levels 1-4; d10 at levels 5-10; d12 at levels 11-16; 2d6 at level 17+

damageTypeChoice.notes: If the attack deals damage, the caster chooses Force damage or the weapon's normal damage type.

attackAugments.notes: Captures Shillelagh's weapon attack modification without replacing the duration and ending-condition fields.

conditionalEndings.description: The weapon enchantment ends early if the caster casts this spell again.
The weapon enchantment ends early if the caster lets go of the weapon.

shillelagh.description: A club or quarterstaff you are holding is imbued with nature's power. For the duration, you can use your spellcasting ability instead of Strength for the attack and damage rolls of melee attacks using that weapon, and the weapon's damage die becomes a d8. If the attack deals damage, it can be Force damage or the weapon's normal damage type (your choice). The spell ends early if you cast it again or if you let go of the weapon.
shillelagh.higherLevels: The damage die changes when you reach levels 5 (d10), 11 (d12), and 17 (2d6).

shining-smite.name: Shining Smite

effects.description: The target hit by the triggering strike takes an extra 2d6 Radiant damage.
Until the spell ends, the target sheds Bright Light in a 5-foot radius.

shining-smite.description: The target hit by the strike takes an extra 2d6 Radiant damage from the attack. Until the spell ends, the target sheds Bright Light in a 5-foot radius, attack rolls against it have Advantage, and it can't benefit from the Invisible condition.
shining-smite.higherLevels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 2.

shocking-grasp.name: Shocking Grasp

scaling.customFormula: 1d8 at 1, 2d8 at 5, 3d8 at 11, 4d8 at 17

shocking-grasp.description: Lightning springs from you to a creature that you try to touch. Make a melee spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 Lightning damage, and it can't make Opportunity Attacks until the start of its next turn.
shocking-grasp.higherLevels: Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d8 when you reach levels 5 (2d8), 11 (3d8), and 17 (4d8).

sickening-radiance.name: Sickening Radiance

effects.description: A creature that moves into the spell's area for the first time on a turn makes a Constitution save, taking no damage on a success.
A creature that starts its turn in the spell's area makes a Constitution save, taking no damage on a success.
Dim, greenish light fills the 30-foot-radius Sphere, spreads around corners, and causes failed-save creatures to emit Dim Light in a 5-foot radius.

sickening-radiance.description: Dim, greenish light spreads within a 30-foot-radius sphere centered on a point you choose within range. The light spreads around corners, and it lasts until the spell ends. When a creature moves into the spell's area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, that creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take 4d10 radiant damage, suffer one level of exhaustion, and emit a dim, greenish light in a 5-foot radius. This light makes it impossible for the creature to benefit from being invisible. The light and any levels of exhaustion caused by this spell go away when the spell ends.

silence.name: Silence

effects.description: Creates a silence zone: blocks sound, deafens creatures inside, prevents verbal casting, and grants immunity to thunder damage within.
Creatures entirely inside the silence sphere are Deafened while the spell persists.
Creatures and objects entirely inside the silence sphere have immunity to Thunder damage.

silence.description: For the duration, no sound can be created within or pass through a 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point you choose within range. Any creature or object entirely inside the Sphere has Immunity to Thunder damage, and creatures have the Deafened condition while entirely inside it. Casting a spell that includes a Verbal component is impossible there.

silent-image.name: Silent Image

effects.description: Creates and moves a silent, intangible image up to a 15-foot cube; Investigation check (action) reveals the illusion.

silent-image.description: You create the image of an object, a creature, or some other visible phenomenon that is no larger than a 15-foot Cube . The image appears at a spot within range and lasts for the duration. The image is purely visual; it isn't accompanied by sound, smell, or other sensory effects. As a Magic action, you can cause the image to move to any spot within range. As the image changes location, you can alter its appearance so that its movements appear natural for the image. For example, if you create an image of a creature and move it, you can alter the image so that it appears to be walking. Physical interaction with the image reveals it to be an illusion, since things can pass through it. A creature that takes a Study action to examine the image can determine that it is an illusion with a successful Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC. If a creature discerns the illusion for what it is, the creature can see through the image.

simulacrum.name: Simulacrum

components.materialDescription: powdered ruby worth 1,500+ GP, which the spell consumes

formOptions: Simulacrum of one Beast or Humanoid

effects.description: Creates a Construct simulacrum using the original creature statistics, with half Hit Point maximum, command obedience, turn sharing, and rest/level restrictions.
Damaged simulacrum Hit Points can be restored only by repair during the caster Long Rest, expending components worth 100 GP per Hit Point restored while the simulacrum stays within 5 feet.

You create a simulacrum of one Beast or Humanoid that is within 10 feet of you for the entire casting of the spell. You finish the casting by touching both the creature and a pile of ice or snow that is the same size as that creature, and the pile turns into the simulacrum, which is a creature. It uses the game statistics of the original creature at the time of casting, except it is a Construct, its Hit Point maximum is half as much, and it can't cast this spell. The simulacrum is Friendly to you and creatures you designate. It obeys your commands and acts on your turn in combat. The simulacrum can't gain levels, and it can't take Short or Long Rests. If the simulacrum takes damage, the only way to restore its Hit Points is to repair it as you take a Long Rest, during which you expend components worth 100 GP per Hit Point restored. The simulacrum must stay within 5 feet of you for the repair. The simulacrum lasts until it drops to 0 Hit Points, at which point it reverts to snow and melts away. If you cast this spell again, any simulacrum you created with this spell is instantly destroyed.

controlOptions.name: Beast Or Humanoid Target
controlOptions.effect: The copied creature must be one Beast or Humanoid.

controlOptions.name: Ten Foot Casting Presence
controlOptions.effect: The copied creature must be within 10 feet of you for the entire casting.

controlOptions.name: Ice Or Snow Body
controlOptions.effect: You finish by touching the creature and a same-size pile of ice or snow, which turns into the simulacrum.

controlOptions.name: Construct Copy
controlOptions.effect: The simulacrum uses the original creature's statistics except it is a Construct.

controlOptions.name: Half Hit Point Maximum
controlOptions.effect: The simulacrum's Hit Point maximum is half the original creature's.

controlOptions.name: Cannot Cast Simulacrum
controlOptions.effect: The simulacrum cannot cast this spell.

controlOptions.name: Friendly To Designated Creatures
controlOptions.effect: The simulacrum is Friendly to you and creatures you designate.

controlOptions.name: Obeys Commands
controlOptions.effect: The simulacrum obeys your commands.

controlOptions.name: Acts On Your Turn
controlOptions.effect: The simulacrum acts on your turn in combat.

controlOptions.name: Cannot Gain Levels
controlOptions.effect: The simulacrum cannot gain levels.

controlOptions.name: Cannot Rest
controlOptions.effect: The simulacrum cannot take Short or Long Rests.

controlOptions.name: Repair During Long Rest
controlOptions.effect: If damaged, the simulacrum can restore Hit Points only by repair as you take a Long Rest.

controlOptions.name: Repair Cost Per HP
controlOptions.effect: Repair requires components worth 100 GP per Hit Point restored.

controlOptions.name: Repair Proximity
controlOptions.effect: The simulacrum must stay within 5 feet of you for repair.

controlOptions.name: Drops To Zero Reverts To Snow
controlOptions.effect: When it drops to 0 Hit Points, it reverts to snow and melts away.

controlOptions.name: Recast Destroys Existing Simulacrum
controlOptions.effect: If you cast this spell again, any simulacrum you created with this spell is instantly destroyed.

simulacrum.description: You create a simulacrum of one Beast or Humanoid that is within 10 feet of you for the entire casting of the spell. You finish the casting by touching both the creature and a pile of ice or snow that is the same size as that creature, and the pile turns into the simulacrum, which is a creature. It uses the game statistics of the original creature at the time of casting, except it is a Construct, its Hit Point maximum is half as much, and it can't cast this spell. The simulacrum is Friendly to you and creatures you designate. It obeys your commands and acts on your turn in combat. The simulacrum can't gain levels, and it can't take Short or Long Rests. If the simulacrum takes damage, the only way to restore its Hit Points is to repair it as you take a Long Rest, during which you expend components worth 100 GP per Hit Point restored. The simulacrum must stay within 5 feet of you for the repair. The simulacrum lasts until it drops to 0 Hit Points, at which point it reverts to snow and melts away. If you cast this spell again, any simulacrum you created with this spell is instantly destroyed.

skill-empowerment.name: Skill Empowerment

effects.description: Your magic deepens a creature's understanding of its own talent. You touch one willing creature and give it expertise in one skill of your choice; until the spell ends, the creature doubles its proficiency bonus for ability checks it makes that use the chosen skill. You must choose a skill in which the target is proficient and that isn't already benefiting from an effect, such as Expertise, that doubles its proficiency bonus.

skill-empowerment.description: Your magic deepens a creature's understanding of its own talent. You touch one willing creature and give it expertise in one skill of your choice; until the spell ends, the creature doubles its proficiency bonus for ability checks it makes that use the chosen skill. You must choose a skill in which the target is proficient and that isn't already benefiting from an effect, such as Expertise, that doubles its proficiency bonus.

skywrite.name: Skywrite

effects.description: Creates up to ten words of cloud-writing in the sky, visible up to 50 miles, lasting for the duration unless dispersed by strong wind.

conditionalEndings.description: A strong wind can disperse the cloud words and end the spell early.

skywrite.description: You cause up to ten words to form in a part of the sky you can see. The words appear to be made of cloud and remain in place for the spell's duration. The words dissipate when the spell ends. A strong wind can disperse the clouds and end the spell early.

sleep.name: Sleep

components.materialDescription: a pinch of sand or rose petals

areaTargetSelection.notes: The caster chooses which creatures in the 5-foot-radius Sphere are affected; unchosen creatures in the area are not affected.

effects.description: On a failed Wisdom save, a chosen creature in the area is Incapacitated until the end of its next turn, then repeats the save; a failed second save makes it Unconscious for the duration. Ends on that target if it takes damage or is shaken awake.
If a creature Incapacitated by this spell fails the repeat Wisdom save at the end of its next turn, it becomes Unconscious for the spell's duration.

sleep.description: Each creature of your choice in a 5-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point within range must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the Incapacitated condition until the end of its next turn, at which point it must repeat the save. If the target fails the second save, the target has the Unconscious condition for the duration. The spell ends on a target if it takes damage or someone within 5 feet of it takes an action to shake it out of the spell's effect. Creatures that don't sleep, such as elves, or that have Immunity to the Exhaustion condition automatically succeed on saves against this spell.

sleet-storm.name: Sleet Storm
sleet-storm.description: Until the spell ends, sleet falls in a 40-foot-tall, 20-foot-radius Cylinder centered on a point you choose within range. The area is Heavily Obscured , and exposed flames in the area are doused. Ground in the Cylinder is Difficult Terrain . When a creature enters the Cylinder for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, it must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or have the Prone condition and lose Concentration .

slow.name: Slow

effects.description: You alter time around up to six creatures of your choice in a 40-foot Cube within range. Each target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be affected by this spell for the duration. An affected target's Speed is halved, it takes a -2 penalty to AC and Dexterity saving throws, and it can't take Reactions. On its turns, it can take either an action or a Bonus Action, not both, and it can make only one attack if it takes the Attack action. If it casts a spell with a Somatic component, there is a 25 percent chance the spell fails as a result of the target making the spell's gestures too slowly. An affected target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the spell on itself on a success.

recurringMechanics.notes: An affected target repeats the Wisdom save at the end of each of its turns, ending Slow on itself on a success.

slow.description: You alter time around up to six creatures of your choice in a 40-foot Cube within range. Each target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be affected by this spell for the duration. An affected target's Speed is halved, it takes a -2 penalty to AC and Dexterity saving throws, and it can't take Reactions. On its turns, it can take either an action or a Bonus Action, not both, and it can make only one attack if it takes the Attack action. If it casts a spell with a Somatic component, there is a 25 percent chance the spell fails as a result of the target making the spell's gestures too slowly. An affected target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the spell on itself on a success.

snare.name: Snare

components.materialDescription: 25 feet of rope, which the spell consumes

conditionalEndings.description: After the trap is triggered, the spell ends when no creature is restrained by it.

effects.description: Trap is nearly invisible and can be discerned with Intelligence (Investigation) against your spell save DC. A restrained creature or another creature that can reach it can use an action to make an Intelligence (Arcana) check against your spell save DC, ending the restrained effect on a success.

snare.description: As you cast this spell, you use the rope to create a circle with a 5-foot radius on the ground or the floor. When you finish casting, the rope disappears and the circle becomes a magic trap. This trap is nearly invisible, requiring a successful Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC to be discerned. The trap triggers when a Small, Medium, or Large creature moves onto the ground or the floor in the spell's radius. That creature must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or be magically hoisted into the air, leaving it hanging upside down 3 feet above the ground or the floor. The creature is restrained there until the spell ends. A restrained creature can make a Dexterity saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Alternatively, the creature or someone else who can reach it can use an action to make an Intelligence (Arcana) check against your spell save DC. On a success, the restrained effect ends. After the trap is triggered, the spell ends when no creature is restrained by it.

soul-cage.name: Soul Cage

components.materialDescription: a tiny silver cage worth 100 gp

effects.description: Steal Life: the caster can use a bonus action to drain vigor from the trapped soul and regain 2d8 Hit Points.

This spell snatches the soul of a humanoid as it dies and traps it inside the tiny cage you use for the material component. A stolen soul remains inside the cage until the spell ends or until you destroy the cage, which ends the spell. While you have a soul inside the cage, you can exploit it in any of the ways described below. You can use a trapped soul up to six times. Once you exploit a soul for the sixth time, it is released, and the spell ends. While a soul is trapped, the dead humanoid it came from can't be revived.

controlOptions.name: Steal Life
controlOptions.effect: The caster can use a bonus action to regain 2d8 Hit Points by draining vigor from the soul.

controlOptions.name: Query Soul
controlOptions.effect: The caster asks the soul a question without an action and receives a brief truthful telepathic answer understandable regardless of language.

controlOptions.name: Borrow Experience
controlOptions.effect: The caster can use a bonus action to gain advantage on the next attack roll, ability check, or saving throw before the start of the caster's next turn.

controlOptions.name: Eyes of the Dead
controlOptions.effect: The caster can use an action to create an invisible sensor in a place the humanoid saw in life on the caster's current plane, concentrating up to 10 minutes.

controlOptions.name: Six Uses
controlOptions.effect: The trapped soul can be exploited up to six times, then it is released and the spell ends.

controlOptions.name: Prevent Revival
controlOptions.effect: While the soul is trapped, the dead humanoid it came from cannot be revived.

controlOptions.name: Destroy Cage Ends Spell
controlOptions.effect: Destroying the cage releases the soul and ends the spell.

soul-cage.description: This spell snatches the soul of a humanoid as it dies and traps it inside the tiny cage you use for the material component. A stolen soul remains inside the cage until the spell ends or until you destroy the cage, which ends the spell. While you have a soul inside the cage, you can exploit it in any of the ways described below. You can use a trapped soul up to six times. Once you exploit a soul for the sixth time, it is released, and the spell ends. While a soul is trapped, the dead humanoid it came from can't be revived. Steal Life. You can use a bonus action to drain vigor from the soul and regain 2d8 hit points. Query Soul. You ask the soul a question (no action required) and receive a brief telepathic answer, which you can understand regardless of the language used. The soul knows only what it knew in life, but it must answer you truthfully and to the best of its ability. The answer is no more than a sentence or two and might be cryptic. Borrow Experience. You can use a bonus action to bolster yourself with the soul's life experience, making your next attack roll, ability check, or saving throw with advantage. If you don't use this benefit before the start of your next turn, it is lost. Eyes of the Dead. You can use an action to name a place the humanoid saw in life, which creates an invisible sensor somewhere in that place if it is on the plane of existence you're currently on. The sensor remains for as long as you concentrate, up to 10 minutes (as if you were concentrating on a spell). You receive visual and auditory information from the sensor as if you were in its space using your senses. A creature that can see the sensor (such as one using see invisibility or truesight) sees a translucent image of the tormented humanoid whose soul you caged. * - which you take when a humanoid you can see within 60 feet of you dies

spare-the-dying.name: Spare the Dying

effects.description: Stabilize a creature at 0 HP; they are no longer dying.
The target at 0 Hit Points becomes Stable.

spare-the-dying.description: Choose a creature within range that has 0 Hit Points and isn't dead. The creature becomes Stable.
spare-the-dying.higherLevels: The range doubles when you reach levels 5 (30 feet), 11 (60 feet), and 17 (120 feet).

speak-with-animals.name: Speak with Animals

effects.description: Communicate verbally with beasts for the duration; their knowledge is limited by intelligence and disposition.

speak-with-animals.description: For the duration, you can comprehend and verbally communicate with Beasts, and you can use any of the Influence action's skill options with them. Most Beasts have little to say about topics that don't pertain to survival or companionship, but at minimum, a Beast can give you information about nearby locations and monsters, including whatever it has perceived within the past day.

speak-with-dead.name: Speak with Dead

effects.description: You grant the semblance of life to a corpse of your choice within range, allowing it to answer questions you pose. The corpse must have a mouth, and this spell fails if the deceased creature was Undead when it died. The spell also fails if the corpse was the target of this spell within the past 10 days. Until the spell ends, you can ask the corpse up to five questions. The corpse knows only what it knew in life, including the languages it knew. Answers are usually brief, cryptic, or repetitive, and the corpse is under no compulsion to offer a truthful answer if you are antagonistic toward it or it recognizes you as an enemy. This spell doesn't return the creature's soul to its body, only its animating spirit. Thus, the corpse can't learn new information, doesn't comprehend anything that has happened since it died, and can't speculate about future events.

speak-with-dead.description: You grant the semblance of life to a corpse of your choice within range, allowing it to answer questions you pose. The corpse must have a mouth, and this spell fails if the deceased creature was Undead when it died. The spell also fails if the corpse was the target of this spell within the past 10 days. Until the spell ends, you can ask the corpse up to five questions. The corpse knows only what it knew in life, including the languages it knew. Answers are usually brief, cryptic, or repetitive, and the corpse is under no compulsion to offer a truthful answer if you are antagonistic toward it or it recognizes you as an enemy. This spell doesn't return the creature's soul to its body, only its animating spirit. Thus, the corpse can't learn new information, doesn't comprehend anything that has happened since it died, and can't speculate about future events.

speak-with-plants.name: Speak with Plants

effects.description: You imbue plants within 30 feet of you with limited sentience and animation, giving them the ability to communicate with you and follow your simple commands. You can question plants about events in the spell's area within the past day, gaining information about creatures that have passed, weather, and other circumstances. You can also turn difficult terrain caused by plant growth (such as thickets and undergrowth) into ordinary terrain that lasts for the duration. Or you can turn ordinary terrain where plants are present into difficult terrain that lasts for the duration, causing vines and branches to hinder pursuers, for example. Plants might be able to perform other tasks on your behalf, at the GM's discretion. The spell doesn't enable plants to uproot themselves and move about, but they can freely move branches, tendrils, and stalks. If a plant creature is in the area, you can communicate with it as if you shared a common language, but you gain no magical ability to influence it. This spell can cause the plants created by the Entangle spell to release a restrained creature.

speak-with-plants.description: You imbue plants in an immobile 30-foot Emanation with limited sentience and animation, giving them the ability to communicate with you and follow your simple commands. You can question plants about events in the spell's area within the past day, gaining information about creatures that have passed, weather, and other circumstances. You can also turn Difficult Terrain caused by plant growth (such as thickets and undergrowth) into ordinary terrain that lasts for the duration. Or you can turn ordinary terrain where plants are present into Difficult Terrain that lasts for the duration. The spell doesn't enable plants to uproot themselves and move about, but they can move their branches, tendrils, and stalks for you. If a Plant creature is in the area, you can communicate with it as if you shared a common language.

spider-climb.name: Spider Climb

components.materialDescription: a drop of bitumen and a spider

effects.description: Until the spell ends, one willing creature you touch gains the ability to move up, down, and across vertical surfaces and upside down along ceilings, while leaving its hands free. The target also gains a climbing speed equal to its walking speed.

spider-climb.description: Until the spell ends, one willing creature you touch gains the ability to move up, down, and across vertical surfaces and along ceilings, while leaving its hands free. The target also gains a Climb Speed equal to its Speed.
spider-climb.higherLevels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 2.

spike-growth.name: Spike Growth

effects.description: Difficult terrain; creatures take damage per 5 ft traveled within the area; camouflaged (Perception vs save DC to notice).

spike-growth.description: The ground in a 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point within range sprouts hard spikes and thorns. The area becomes Difficult Terrain for the duration. When a creature moves into or within the area, it takes 2d4 Piercing damage for every 5 feet it travels. The transformation of the ground is camouflaged to look natural. Any creature that can't see the area when the spell is cast must take a Search action and succeed on a Wisdom (Perception or Survival) check against your spell save DC to recognize the terrain as hazardous before entering it.

spirit-guardians.name: Spirit Guardians
spirit-guardians.description: Protective spirits flit around you in a 15-foot Emanation for the duration. If you are good or neutral, their spectral form appears angelic or fey (your choice). If you are evil, they appear fiendish. When you cast this spell, you can designate creatures to be unaffected by it. Any other creature's Speed is halved in the Emanation, and whenever the Emanation enters a creature's space and whenever a creature enters the Emanation or ends its turn there, the creature must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes 3d8 Radiant damage (if you are good or neutral) or 3d8 Necrotic damage (if you are evil). On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage. A creature makes this save only once per turn.
spirit-guardians.higherLevels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 3.

spiritual-weapon.name: Spiritual Weapon

effects.description: Initial melee spell attack when the weapon appears.
Bonus action each turn to move the weapon up to 20 ft and attack.

scaling.customFormula: Damage increases by 1d8 at slots 3/4, and another 1d8 at 5/6, etc.

spiritual-weapon.description: You create a floating, spectral force that resembles a weapon of your choice and lasts for the duration. The force appears within range in a space of your choice, and you can immediately make one melee spell attack against one creature within 5 feet of the force. On a hit, the target takes Force damage equal to 1d8 plus your spellcasting ability modifier. As a Bonus Action on your later turns, you can move the force up to 20 feet and repeat the attack against a creature within 5 feet of it.
spiritual-weapon.higherLevels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for every slot level above 2.

staggering-smite.name: Staggering Smite

effects.description: The next time the caster hits a creature with a melee weapon attack during the spell's duration, the attack deals an extra 4d6 Psychic damage.

attackRollModifier.notes: On a failed Wisdom save after the triggering hit, the target has disadvantage on attack rolls until the end of its next turn.

effects.description: On a failed Wisdom save after the triggering hit, the target has disadvantage on attack rolls until the end of its next turn.
See description.

staggering-smite.description: The next time you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack during this spell's duration, your weapon pierces both body and mind, and the attack deals an extra 4d6 psychic damage to the target. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks, and can't take reactions, until the end of its next turn.

starry-wisp.name: Starry Wisp

scaling.customFormula: 1d8 at 1, 2d8 at 5, 3d8 at 11, 4d8 at 17

effects.description: Target sheds dim light in a 10-foot radius and cannot benefit from invisibility until end of your next turn.

starry-wisp.description: You launch a mote of light at one creature or object within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 Radiant damage, and until the end of your next turn, it emits Dim Light in a 10-foot radius and can't benefit from the Invisible condition.
starry-wisp.higherLevels: This spell's damage increases by 1d8 when you reach 5th level (2d8), 11th level (3d8), and 17th level (4d8).

steel-wind-strike.name: Steel Wind Strike

components.materialDescription: a melee weapon worth at least 1 sp

effects.description: Make a melee spell attack against each selected target; on a hit, the target takes 6d10 Force damage.
After resolving the attacks, the caster can teleport to an unoccupied visible space within 5 feet of one target hit or missed.

steel-wind-strike.description: You flourish the weapon used in the casting and then vanish to strike like the wind. Choose up to five creatures you can see within range. Make a melee spell attack against each target. On a hit, a target takes 6d10 force damage. You can then teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 5 feet of one of the targets you hit or missed.

stinking-cloud.name: Stinking Cloud
stinking-cloud.description: You create a 20-foot-radius Sphere of yellow, nauseating gas centered on a point within range. The cloud is Heavily Obscured . The cloud lingers in the air for the duration or until a strong wind (such as the one created by Gust of Wind) disperses it. Each creature that starts its turn in the Sphere must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have the Poisoned condition until the end of the current turn. While Poisoned in this way, the creature can't take an action or a Bonus Action.

stone-shape.name: Stone Shape

effects.description: You touch a stone object of Medium size or smaller or a section of stone no more than 5 feet in any dimension and form it into any shape you like. For example, you could shape a large rock into a weapon, statue, or coffer, or you could make a small passage through a wall that is 5 feet thick. You could also shape a stone door or its frame to seal the door shut. The object you create can have up to two hinges and a latch, but finer mechanical detail isn't possible.

stone-shape.description: You touch a stone object of Medium size or smaller or a section of stone no more than 5 feet in any dimension and form it into any shape you like. For example, you could shape a large rock into a weapon, statue, or coffer, or you could make a small passage through a wall that is 5 feet thick. You could also shape a stone door or its frame to seal the door shut. The object you create can have up to two hinges and a latch, but finer mechanical detail isn't possible.

stoneskin.name: Stoneskin

effects.description: Until the spell ends, one willing creature the caster touches has Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.

stoneskin.description: Until the spell ends, one willing creature you touch has Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.

storm-of-vengeance.name: Storm of Vengeance

effects.description: When the storm appears, creatures under the cloud save against 2d6 Thunder damage.
A creature that fails the initial Constitution save is Deafened for the spell duration.
On Turn 2, creatures and objects under the cloud take 4d6 Acid damage.
On Turn 3, six bolts strike six different creatures or objects under the cloud for 10d6 Lightning damage, Dexterity save for half.
On Turn 4, creatures under the cloud take 2d6 Bludgeoning damage.
On Turns 5 through 10, creatures under the cloud take 1d6 Cold damage.
Until the spell ends, the area under the storm is Difficult Terrain.
Until the spell ends, the area under the storm is Heavily Obscured.

A churning storm cloud forms for the duration, centered on a point within range and spreading to a radius of 300 feet. Each creature under the cloud when it appears must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take 2d6 Thunder damage and have the Deafened condition for the duration. At the start of each of your later turns, the storm produces different effects, as detailed below. Turn 2. Acidic rain falls. Each creature and object under the cloud takes 4d6 Acid damage. Turn 3. You call six bolts of lightning from the cloud to strike six different creatures or objects beneath it. Each target makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 10d6 Lightning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. Turn 4. Hailstones rain down. Each creature under the cloud takes 2d6 Bludgeoning damage. Turns 5-10. Gusts and freezing rain assail the area under the cloud. Each creature there takes 1d6 Cold damage. Until the spell ends, the area is Difficult Terrain and Heavily Obscured , ranged attacks with weapons are impossible there, and strong wind blows through the area.

controlOptions.effect: When the storm appears, creatures under it save against 2d6 Thunder damage and Deafened for the duration.

controlOptions.name: Turn 2 acid rain

controlOptions.name: Turn 4 hailstones
controlOptions.effect: At the start of the caster fourth later turn, creatures under the cloud take 2d6 Bludgeoning damage.

controlOptions.name: Turns 5 through 10 freezing rain
controlOptions.effect: On later turns 5 through 10, creatures under the cloud take 1d6 Cold damage.

controlOptions.name: Area control
controlOptions.effect: Until the spell ends, the area is Difficult Terrain and Heavily Obscured, ranged weapon attacks are impossible there, and strong wind blows through it.

effectSchedule.notes: Initial thunder damage and Deafened resolve when the storm appears; this schedule covers later caster-turn stages only.

At the start of each of your later turns, the storm produces different effects, as detailed below. Turn 2. Acidic rain falls. Each creature and object under the cloud takes 4d6 Acid damage. Turn 3. Until the spell ends, the area is Difficult Terrain and Heavily Obscured , ranged attacks with weapons are impossible there, and strong wind blows through the area.

storm-sphere.name: Storm Sphere

effects.description: Each creature in the sphere when it appears makes a Strength save, taking no damage on a success.
A creature that ends its turn in the sphere makes a Strength save, taking no damage on a success.

As a Bonus Action each turn, the caster can make a ranged spell attack from the sphere's center against one creature within 60 feet of the center. The attack has advantage if the target is in the sphere.
The sphere's space is Difficult Terrain.
Creatures within 30 feet of the sphere have disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks made to listen.

storm-sphere.description: A 20-foot-radius sphere of whirling air springs into existence centered on a point you choose within range. The sphere remains for the spell's duration. Each creature in the sphere when it appears or that ends its turn there must succeed on a Strength saving throw or take 2d6 bludgeoning damage. The sphere's space is difficult terrain. Until the spell ends, you can use a bonus action on each of your turns to cause a bolt of lightning to leap from the center of the sphere toward one creature you choose within 60 feet of the center. Make a ranged spell attack. You have advantage on the attack roll if the target is in the sphere. On a hit, the target takes 4d6 lightning damage. Creatures within 30 feet of the sphere have disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks made to listen.
storm-sphere.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the damage increases for each of its effects by 1d6 for each slot level above 4th.

suggestion.name: Suggestion

effects.description: On failed save, target follows the reasonable suggestion for up to 8 hours; ends if target is damaged or companions harm it.

conditionalEndings.description: The spell ends for the target if the caster or the caster's allies deal damage to that target.
If the suggested activity can be completed before the full duration, the spell ends for the target when that activity is completed.

effects.description: A failed Wisdom save applies the Charmed condition for the spell's duration or until the spell ends early.

conditionalEndings.description: Damage dealt to the target by the caster or the caster's allies ends the spell for that target.
Completing the suggested activity before the full duration ends the spell for that target.

aiContext.prompt: The player is casting Suggestion. Adjudicate the requested command. The suggestion must sound reasonable, be achievable within 8 hours, and not involve anything that would obviously deal damage to the target or its allies. For example, 'drop your weapons and flee' is okay, 'stab yourself' is not. Evaluate if the command meets these criteria. If valid, approve it and optionally define narrative mechanical effects. If invalid, reject it.

suggestion.description: You suggest a course of activity-described in no more than 25 words-to one creature you can see within range that can hear and understand you. The suggestion must sound achievable and not involve anything that would obviously deal damage to the target or its allies. For example, you could say, "Fetch the key to the cult's treasure vault, and give the key to me." Or you could say, "Stop fighting, leave this library peacefully, and don't return." The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the Charmed condition for the duration or until you or your allies deal damage to the target. The Charmed target pursues the suggestion to the best of its ability. The suggested activity can continue for the entire duration, but if the suggested activity can be completed in a shorter time, the spell ends for the target upon completing it.

summon-beast.name: Summon Beast

components.materialDescription: a feather, tuft of fur, and fish tail inside a gilded acorn worth 200+ GP

effects.description: You call forth a bestial spirit. It manifests in an unoccupied space that you can see within range and uses the Bestial Spirit stat block. When you cast the spell, choose an environment: Air, Land, or Water. The creature resembles an animal of your choice that is native to the chosen environment, which determines certain details in its stat block. The creature is an ally to you and your companions. In combat, the creature shares your Initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the Dodge action and uses its move to avoid danger.

conditionalEndings.description: The bestial spirit disappears when it drops to 0 Hit Points.

The creature disappears when it drops to 0 Hit Points or when the spell ends. The creature is an ally to you and your allies. In combat, the creature shares your Initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the Dodge action and uses its movement to avoid danger.

summon-greater-demon.name: Summon Greater Demon

components.materialDescription: a vial of blood from a humanoid killed within the past 24 hours

effects.description: See description.

summon-greater-demon.description: You utter foul words, summoning one demon from the chaos of the Abyss. You choose the demon's type, which must be one of challenge rating 5 or lower, such as a shadow demon or a barlgura. The demon appears in an unoccupied space you can see within range, and the demon disappears when it drops to 0 hit points or when the spell ends. Roll initiative for the demon, which has its own turns. When you summon it and on each of your turns thereafter, you can issue a verbal command to it (requiring no action on your part), telling it what it must do on its next turn. If you issue no command, it spends its turn attacking any creature within reach that has attacked it. At the end of each of the demon's turns, it makes a Charisma saving throw. The demon has disadvantage on this saving throw if you say its true name. On a failed save, the demon continues to obey you. On a successful save, your control of the demon ends for the rest of the duration, and the demon spends its turns pursuing and attacking the nearest non-demons to the best of its ability. If you stop concentrating on the spell before it reaches its full duration, an uncontrolled demon doesn't disappear for 1d6 rounds if it still has hit points. As part of casting the spell, you can form a circle on the ground with the blood used as a material component. The circle is large enough to encompass your space. While the spell lasts, the summoned demon can't cross the circle or harm it, and it can't target anyone within it. Using the material component in this manner consumes it when the spell ends.
summon-greater-demon.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the challenge rating increases by 1 for each slot level above 4th.

summon-lesser-demons.name: Summon Lesser Demons

components.materialDescription: a vial of blood from a humanoid killed within the past 24 hours

effects.description: You utter foul words, summoning demons from the Chaos of the Abyss. You roll on the following table to determine what appears. The GM chooses the demons, such as Manes or Dretches, and you choose the unoccupied spaces you can see within range where they appear. A summoned demon disappears when it drops to 0 hit points or when the spell ends. The demons are hostile to all creatures, including you. Roll initiative for the summoned demons as a group, which has its own turns. The demons pursue and attack the nearest non-demons to the best of their ability. As part of casting the spell, you can form a circle of blood with the material component used to cast the spell. The circle is large enough to encompass your space. While the spell lasts, the summoned demons can't cross the circle or harm it, and they can't target anyone within it. Using the material component in this manner consumes it when the spell ends.

summon-lesser-demons.description: You utter foul words, summoning demons from the chaos of the Abyss. Roll on the following table to determine what appears. d6 Demons Summoned 1-2 Two demons of challenge rating 1 or lower 3-4 Four demons of challenge rating 1/2 or lower 5-6 Eight demons of challenge rating 1/4 or lower The DM chooses the demons, such as manes or dretches, and you choose the unoccupied spaces you can see within range where they appear. A summoned demon disappears when it drops to 0 hit points or when the spell ends. The demons are hostile to all creatures, including you. Roll initiative for the summoned demons as a group, which has its own turns. The demons pursue and attack the nearest non-demons to the best of their ability. As part of casting the spell, you can form a circle on the ground with the blood used as a material component. The circle is large enough to encompass your space. While the spell lasts, the summoned demons can't cross the circle or harm it, and they can't target anyone within it. Using the material component in this manner consumes it when the spell ends.
summon-lesser-demons.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th or 7th level, you summon twice as many demons. If you cast it using a spell slot of 8th or 9th level, you summon three times as many demons.

sunbeam.name: Sunbeam

effects.description: The caster launches or recreates a 5-foot-wide, 60-foot-long Line of radiance; each creature in the Line makes a Constitution save, taking 6d8 Radiant damage on a failed save or half as much on a successful one.
On a failed Constitution save, a creature has the Blinded condition until the start of the caster's next turn.
A mote of brilliant radiance shines above the caster for the duration, shedding Bright Light in a 30-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 30 feet. This light is sunlight.

grantedActions.notes: Until the spell ends, the caster can take a Magic action to create a new Line of radiance.

controlOptions.name: Repeat Sunbeam Line
controlOptions.effect: Until the spell ends, the caster can take a Magic action to create a new Line of radiance.

controlOptions.name: Sunlight
controlOptions.effect: The mote sheds Bright Light and Dim Light, and this light is sunlight.

sunbeam.description: You launch a sunbeam in a 5-foot-wide, 60-foot-long Line . Each creature in the Line makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 6d8 Radiant damage and has the Blinded condition until the start of your next turn. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage only. Until the spell ends, you can take a Magic action to create a new Line of radiance. For the duration, a mote of brilliant radiance shines above you. It sheds Bright Light in a 30-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 30 feet. This light is sunlight.

sunburst.name: Sunburst

effects.description: Each creature in the Sphere makes a Constitution save, taking 12d6 Radiant damage on a failed save or half on a success.
On a failed Constitution save, a creature has the Blinded condition for 1 minute and repeats the save at the end of each turn.

Brilliant sunlight flashes in a 60-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point you choose within range. Each creature in the Sphere makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 12d6 Radiant damage and has the Blinded condition for 1 minute. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage only. A creature Blinded by this spell makes another Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. This spell dispels Darkness in its area that was created by any spell.

controlOptions.name: Brilliant sunlight
controlOptions.effect: Brilliant sunlight flashes in the spell area.

controlOptions.name: Blinding burst
controlOptions.effect: On a failed Constitution save, a creature has the Blinded condition for 1 minute.

controlOptions.name: End-turn save
controlOptions.effect: A creature Blinded by this spell repeats the Constitution save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

controlOptions.name: Darkness dispel
controlOptions.effect: The spell dispels Darkness in its area that was created by any spell.

On a failed save, a creature takes 12d6 Radiant damage and has the Blinded condition for 1 minute. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage only. A creature Blinded by this spell makes another Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. This spell dispels Darkness in its area that was created by any spell.

swift-quiver.name: Swift Quiver

components.materialDescription: a quiver containing at least one piece of ammunition

effects.description: The quiver produces nonmagical ammunition as needed. On each turn until the spell ends, the caster can use a bonus action to make two attacks with a weapon using ammunition from the quiver.

grantedActions.notes: The attacks must use ammunition from the transmuted quiver.

endCleanup.notes: Any ammunition created by Swift Quiver disintegrates when the spell ends.

swift-quiver.description: You transmute your quiver so it produces an endless supply of nonmagical ammunition, which seems to leap into your hand when you reach for it. On each of your turns until the spell ends, you can use a bonus action to make two attacks with a weapon that uses ammunition from the quiver. Each time you make such a ranged attack, your quiver magically replaces the piece of ammunition you used with a similar piece of nonmagical ammunition. Any pieces of ammunition created by this spell disintegrate when the spell ends. If the quiver leaves your possession, the spell ends.

sword-burst.name: Sword Burst

scaling.customFormula: 1d6 at level 1, 2d6 at level 5, 3d6 at level 11, 4d6 at level 17

sword-burst.description: You create a momentary circle of spectral blades that sweep around you. All other creatures within 5 feet of you must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 1d6 force damage.
sword-burst.higherLevels: This spell's damage increases by 1d6 when you reach 5th level (2d6), 11th level (3d6), and 17th level (4d6).

symbol.name: Symbol

components.materialDescription: powdered diamond worth 1,000+ GP, which the spell consumes

measuredDetails.label: Break Distance
measuredDetails.notes: Moving the object farther breaks the symbol.

effects.description: Death symbol: each target makes a Constitution save, taking 10d10 Necrotic damage on a failed save or half as much on success.

statusCondition.name: Frightened
statusCondition.aftermath: While Frightened, the target must move at least 30 feet away from the glyph on each of its turns if able.

effects.description: Fear symbol failed save applies Frightened for 1 minute.
Pain symbol failed save applies Incapacitated for 1 minute.

statusCondition.name: Unconscious
statusCondition.aftermath: A creature can also wake the target by using an action to shake it awake.

effects.description: Sleep symbol failed save applies Unconscious for 10 minutes.
Stunning symbol failed save applies Stunned for 1 minute.

attackRollModifier.notes: Discord also prevents meaningful communication and gives disadvantage on ability checks.

effects.description: Discord symbol failed save causes argument and disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks for 1 minute.

You inscribe a harmful glyph either on a surface (such as a section of floor or wall) or within an object that can be closed (such as a book or chest). The glyph can cover an area no larger than 10 feet in diameter. If you choose an object, it must remain in place; if it is moved more than 10 feet from where you cast this spell, the glyph is broken, and the spell ends without being triggered. The glyph is nearly imperceptible and requires a successful Wisdom (Perception) check against your spell save DC to notice. When you inscribe the glyph, you set its trigger and choose which effect the symbol bears: Death, Discord, Fear, Pain, Sleep, or Stunning. Each one is explained below. Set the Trigger. You decide what triggers the glyph when you cast the spell. For glyphs inscribed on a surface, common triggers include touching or stepping on the glyph, removing another object covering it, or approaching within a certain distance of it. For glyphs inscribed within an object, common triggers include opening that object or seeing the glyph. You can refine the trigger so that only creatures of certain types activate it (for example, the glyph could be set to affect Aberrations). You can also set conditions for creatures that don't trigger the glyph, such as those who say a certain password. Once triggered, the glyph glows, filling a 60-foot-radius Sphere with Dim Light for 10 minutes, after which time the spell ends. Each creature in the Sphere when the glyph activates is targeted by its effect, as is a creature that enters the Sphere for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there. A creature is targeted only once per turn. Death. Each target makes a Constitution saving throw, taking 10d10 Necrotic damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful save. Discord. Each target makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a target argues with other creatures for 1 minute. During this time, it is incapable of meaningful communication and has Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks. Fear. Each target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the Frightened condition for 1 minute. While Frightened, the target must move at least 30 feet away from the glyph on each of its turns, if able. Pain. Each target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have the Incapacitated condition for 1 minute. Sleep. Each target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the Unconscious condition for 10 minutes. A creature awakens if it takes damage or if someone takes an action to shake it awake. Stunning. Each target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the Stunned condition for 1 minute.

controlOptions.name: Surface Or Object Glyph
controlOptions.effect: The glyph can be inscribed on a surface or within a closable object and can cover an area no larger than 10 feet in diameter.

controlOptions.name: Object Movement Breaks Glyph
controlOptions.effect: If an inscribed object moves more than 10 feet from where the spell was cast, the glyph breaks and the spell ends without triggering.

controlOptions.name: Nearly Imperceptible
controlOptions.effect: The glyph requires a successful Wisdom (Perception) check against your spell save DC to notice.

controlOptions.name: Set Trigger
controlOptions.effect: You decide what triggers the glyph when you cast the spell.

controlOptions.name: Refine Trigger
controlOptions.effect: You can refine the trigger so only creatures of certain types activate it.

controlOptions.name: Trigger Exclusions
controlOptions.effect: You can set conditions for creatures that do not trigger the glyph, such as those saying a password.

controlOptions.name: Triggered Dim Light
controlOptions.effect: Once triggered, the glyph glows and fills a 60-foot-radius Sphere with Dim Light for 10 minutes.

controlOptions.name: Once Per Turn Targeting
controlOptions.effect: A creature in the Sphere is targeted when the glyph activates, enters for the first time on a turn, or ends its turn there, but only once per turn.

controlOptions.name: Death Symbol
controlOptions.effect: Constitution save; failed save deals 10d10 Necrotic damage and successful save deals half.

controlOptions.name: Discord Symbol
controlOptions.effect: Wisdom save; failed save makes the target argue for 1 minute, prevents meaningful communication, and gives Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.

controlOptions.name: Fear Symbol
controlOptions.effect: Wisdom save; failed save gives Frightened for 1 minute and the target must move at least 30 feet away from the glyph on each of its turns if able.

controlOptions.name: Pain Symbol
controlOptions.effect: Constitution save; failed save gives Incapacitated for 1 minute.

controlOptions.name: Sleep Symbol
controlOptions.effect: Wisdom save; failed save gives Unconscious for 10 minutes, ending if the target takes damage or another creature uses an action to shake it awake.

controlOptions.name: Stunning Symbol
controlOptions.effect: Wisdom save; failed save gives Stunned for 1 minute.

conditionalEndings.description: If an inscribed object moves more than 10 feet from where the spell was cast, the glyph breaks and the spell ends without triggering.
Once triggered, the symbol glows and remains active for 10 minutes, after which the spell ends.

symbol.description: You inscribe a harmful glyph either on a surface (such as a section of floor or wall) or within an object that can be closed (such as a book or chest). The glyph can cover an area no larger than 10 feet in diameter. If you choose an object, it must remain in place; if it is moved more than 10 feet from where you cast this spell, the glyph is broken, and the spell ends without being triggered. The glyph is nearly imperceptible and requires a successful Wisdom (Perception) check against your spell save DC to notice. When you inscribe the glyph, you set its trigger and choose which effect the symbol bears: Death, Discord, Fear, Pain, Sleep, or Stunning. Each one is explained below. Set the Trigger. You decide what triggers the glyph when you cast the spell. For glyphs inscribed on a surface, common triggers include touching or stepping on the glyph, removing another object covering it, or approaching within a certain distance of it. For glyphs inscribed within an object, common triggers include opening that object or seeing the glyph. You can refine the trigger so that only creatures of certain types activate it (for example, the glyph could be set to affect Aberrations). You can also set conditions for creatures that don't trigger the glyph, such as those who say a certain password. Once triggered, the glyph glows, filling a 60-foot-radius Sphere with Dim Light for 10 minutes, after which time the spell ends. Each creature in the Sphere when the glyph activates is targeted by its effect, as is a creature that enters the Sphere for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there. A creature is targeted only once per turn. Death. Each target makes a Constitution saving throw, taking 10d10 Necrotic damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful save. Discord. Each target makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a target argues with other creatures for 1 minute. During this time, it is incapable of meaningful communication and has Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks. Fear. Each target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the Frightened condition for 1 minute. While Frightened, the target must move at least 30 feet away from the glyph on each of its turns, if able. Pain. Each target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have the Incapacitated condition for 1 minute. Sleep. Each target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the Unconscious condition for 10 minutes. A creature awakens if it takes damage or if someone takes an action to shake it awake. Stunning. Each target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the Stunned condition for 1 minute.

synaptic-static.name: Synaptic Static
synaptic-static.description: You choose a point within range and cause psychic energy to explode there. Each creature in a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on that point must make an Intelligence saving throw. A creature with an Intelligence score of 2 or lower can't be affected by this spell. A target takes 8d6 psychic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. After a failed save, a target has muddled thoughts for 1 minute. During that time, it rolls a d6 and subtracts the number rolled from all its attack rolls and ability checks, as well as its Constitution saving throws to maintain concentration. The target can make an Intelligence saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

tashas-caustic-brew.name: Tasha's Caustic Brew

effects.description: On a failed Dexterity save, the creature is covered in acid until the spell ends or a creature uses its action to scrape or wash the acid off.
A creature covered in the acid takes this damage at the start of each of its turns.

tashas-caustic-brew.description: A stream of acid emanates from you in a line 30 feet long and 5 feet wide in a direction you choose. Each creature in the line must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or be covered in acid for the spell's duration or until a creature uses its action to scrape or wash the acid off itself or another creature. A creature covered in the acid takes 2d4 acid damage at start of each of its turns.
tashas-caustic-brew.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 2d4 for each slot level above 1st.

tashas-hideous-laughter.name: Tasha's Hideous Laughter

effects.description: Target is incapacitated on a failed save; repeats Wisdom saves at end of turns and after taking damage (with Advantage if damage triggered) to end the effect.
Target is prone on the same failed save and cannot end the Prone condition on itself while the spell lasts.

tashas-hideous-laughter.description: One creature of your choice that you can see within range makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it has the Prone and Incapacitated conditions for the duration. During that time, it laughs uncontrollably if it's capable of laughter, and it can't end the Prone condition on itself. At the end of each of its turns and each time it takes damage, it makes another Wisdom saving throw. The target has Advantage on the save if the save is triggered by damage. On a successful save, the spell ends.
tashas-hideous-laughter.higherLevels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

telekinesis.name: Telekinesis

effects.description: A Huge or smaller creature that fails the Strength save is Restrained until the end of the caster's next turn.

telekinesis.description: You gain the ability to move or manipulate creatures or objects by thought. When you cast the spell and as a Magic action on your later turns before the spell ends, you can exert your will on one creature or object that you can see within range, causing the appropriate effect below. You can affect the same target round after round or choose a new one at any time. If you switch targets, the prior target is no longer affected by the spell. Creature. You can try to move a Huge or smaller creature. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw, or you move it up to 30 feet in any direction within the spell's range. Until the end of your next turn, the creature has the Restrained condition, and if you lift it into the air, it is suspended there. It falls at the end of your next turn unless you use this option on it again and it fails the save. Object. You can try to move a Huge or smaller object. If the object isn't being worn or carried, you automatically move it up to 30 feet in any direction within the spell's range. If the object is worn or carried by a creature, that creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw, or you pull the object away and move it up to 30 feet in any direction within the spell's range. You can exert fine control on objects with your telekinetic grip, such as manipulating a simple tool, opening a door or a container, stowing or retrieving an item from an open container, or pouring the contents from a vial.

telepathy.name: Telepathy

components.materialDescription: a pair of linked silver rings

effects.description: You create a telepathic link between yourself and a willing creature with which you are familiar. The creature can be anywhere on the same plane of existence as you. The spell ends if you or the target are no longer on the same plane. Until the spell ends, you and the target can instantly share words, images, sounds, and other sensory messages with each other through the link, and the target recognizes you as the creature it is communicating with. The spell enables a creature to understand the meaning of your words and any sensory messages you send to it.

controlOptions.name: Familiar willing target
controlOptions.effect: The link is created with one willing creature familiar to the caster.

controlOptions.name: Same-plane limit
controlOptions.effect: The target can be anywhere on the same plane, and the spell ends if caster and target are no longer on the same plane.

controlOptions.name: Shared sensory messages
controlOptions.effect: Caster and target can instantly share words, images, sounds, and other sensory messages through the link.

controlOptions.name: Recognized communicator
controlOptions.effect: The target recognizes the caster as the creature it is communicating with.

controlOptions.name: Meaning comprehension
controlOptions.effect: The spell enables the target to understand the meaning of words and sensory messages sent by the caster.

telepathy.description: You create a telepathic link between yourself and a willing creature with which you are familiar. The creature can be anywhere on the same plane of existence as you. The spell ends if you or the target are no longer on the same plane. Until the spell ends, you and the target can instantly share words, images, sounds, and other sensory messages with each other through the link, and the target recognizes you as the creature it is communicating with. The spell enables a creature to understand the meaning of your words and any sensory messages you send to it.

teleport.name: Teleport

forcedMovement.maxDistance: known same-plane destination selected by the caster

effects.description: Instantly transports the caster and up to eight willing visible creatures, or one eligible object, to a known destination on the same plane.
Mishap outcome: each teleporting creature or target object takes 3d10 Force damage and the DM rerolls on the table; multiple mishaps can occur.

This spell instantly transports you and up to eight willing creatures that you can see within range, or a single object that you can see within range, to a destination you select. If you target an object, it must be Large or smaller, and it can't be held or carried by an unwilling creature. The destination you choose must be known to you, and it must be on the same plane of existence as you. Your familiarity with the destination determines whether you arrive there successfully. The DM rolls 1d100 and consults the Teleportation Outcome table and the explanations after it. Familiarity. Here are the meanings of the terms in the table's Familiarity column: Mishap. The spell's unpredictable magic results in a difficult journey. Each teleporting creature (or the target object) takes 3d10 Force damage, and the DM rerolls on the table to see where you wind up (multiple mishaps can occur, dealing damage each time). Similar Area. You and your group (or the target object) appear in a different area that's visually or thematically similar to the target area. You appear in the closest similar place. If you are heading for your home laboratory, for example, you might appear in another person's laboratory in the same city. Off Target. You and your group (or the target object) appear 2d12 miles away from the destination in a random direction. Roll 1d8 for the direction: 1, east; 2, southeast; 3, south; 4, southwest; 5, west; 6, northwest; 7, north; or 8, northeast. On Target. You and your group (or the target object) appear where you intended.

controlOptions.name: Up To Eight Willing Creatures
controlOptions.effect: You and up to eight willing creatures you can see within range can be transported.

controlOptions.name: Single Object
controlOptions.effect: Alternatively, a single object you can see within range can be transported.

controlOptions.name: Object Size And Carried Limit
controlOptions.effect: A target object must be Large or smaller and cannot be held or carried by an unwilling creature.

controlOptions.name: Known Same Plane Destination
controlOptions.effect: The destination must be known to you and on the same plane of existence as you.

controlOptions.name: Permanent Circle
controlOptions.effect: A permanent teleportation circle whose sigil sequence you know arrives on target.

controlOptions.name: Linked Object
controlOptions.effect: Possessing an object taken from the desired destination within the last six months arrives on target.

controlOptions.name: Very Familiar
controlOptions.effect: A place visited often, carefully studied, or visible when casting uses the Very Familiar outcome row.

controlOptions.name: Seen Casually
controlOptions.effect: A place seen more than once but not very familiar uses the Seen Casually outcome row.

controlOptions.name: Viewed Once Or Described
controlOptions.effect: A place seen once, possibly by magic, or known through another description uses the Viewed Once Or Described row.

controlOptions.name: False Destination
controlOptions.effect: A destination that does not exist uses the False Destination outcome row.

controlOptions.name: Mishap
controlOptions.effect: Each teleporting creature or target object takes 3d10 Force damage, then the DM rerolls on the table; multiple mishaps can occur.

controlOptions.name: Similar Area
controlOptions.effect: The targets appear in the closest visually or thematically similar area.

controlOptions.name: Off Target
controlOptions.effect: The targets appear 2d12 miles away from the destination in a random d8 direction.

controlOptions.name: On Target
controlOptions.effect: The targets appear where intended.

If you target an object, it must be Large or smaller, and it can't be held or carried by an unwilling creature. The destination you choose must be known to you, and it must be on the same plane of existence as you. Your familiarity with the destination determines whether you arrive there successfully.
