teleportation-circle.name: Teleportation Circle

components.materialDescription: rare inks worth 50+ GP, which the spell consumes

effects.description: As you cast the spell, you draw a 5-foot-radius circle on the ground inscribed with sigils that link your location to a permanent teleportation circle of your choice whose sigil sequence you know and that is on the same plane of existence as you. A shimmering portal opens within the circle you drew and remains open until the end of your next turn. Any creature that enters the portal instantly appears within 5 feet of the destination circle or in the nearest unoccupied space if that space is occupied. Many major temples, guildhalls, and other important places have permanent teleportation circles. Each circle includes a unique sigil sequence-a string of runes arranged in a particular pattern. When you first gain the ability to cast this spell, you learn the sigil sequences for two destinations on the Material Plane, determined by the DM. You might learn additional sigil sequences during your adventures. You can commit a new sigil sequence to memory after studying it for 1 minute. You can create a permanent teleportation circle by casting this spell in the same location every day for 365 days.

teleportation-circle.description: As you cast the spell, you draw a 5-foot-radius circle on the ground inscribed with sigils that link your location to a permanent teleportation circle of your choice whose sigil sequence you know and that is on the same plane of existence as you. A shimmering portal opens within the circle you drew and remains open until the end of your next turn. Any creature that enters the portal instantly appears within 5 feet of the destination circle or in the nearest unoccupied space if that space is occupied. Many major temples, guildhalls, and other important places have permanent teleportation circles. Each circle includes a unique sigil sequence-a string of runes arranged in a particular pattern. When you first gain the ability to cast this spell, you learn the sigil sequences for two destinations on the Material Plane, determined by the DM. You might learn additional sigil sequences during your adventures. You can commit a new sigil sequence to memory after studying it for 1 minute. You can create a permanent teleportation circle by casting this spell in the same location every day for 365 days.

temple-of-the-gods.name: Temple of the Gods

components.materialDescription: a holy symbol worth at least 5 gp

forms.label: Temple Footprint
forms.notes: The temple must fit within this cube of space.

statusCondition.name: Entry Blocked
statusCondition.aftermath: On a failed save, a chosen-type creature cannot enter the temple for 24 hours.

effects.description: A chosen-type creature attempting to enter must make a Charisma save; on a failed save it cannot enter the temple for 24 hours.

attackRollModifier.notes: Chosen-type creatures inside subtract 1d4 from attack rolls, ability checks, and saving throws.

effects.description: Chosen-type creatures that can enter subtract 1d4 from attack rolls, ability checks, and saving throws inside the temple.
Whenever a creature in the temple regains hit points from a spell of 1st level or higher, it regains additional hit points equal to the caster Wisdom modifier, minimum 1.

You cause a temple to shimmer into existence on ground you can see within range. The temple must fit within an unoccupied cube of space, up to 120 feet on each side. The temple remains until the spell ends. It is dedicated to whatever god, pantheon, or philosophy is represented by the holy symbol used in the casting. You make all decisions about the temple's appearance. The interior is enclosed by a floor, walls, and a roof, with one door granting access to the interior and as many windows as you wish. Only you and any creatures you designate when you cast the spell can open or close the door. The temple's interior is an open space with an idol or altar at one end. You decide whether the temple is illuminated and whether that illumination is bright light or dim light. The smell of burning incense fills the air within, and the temperature is mild. The temple opposes types of creatures you choose when you cast this spell. Choose one or more of the following: celestials, elementals, fey, fiends, or undead. If a creature of the chosen type attempts to enter the temple, that creature must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, it can't enter the temple for 24 hours. Even if the creature can enter the temple, the magic there hinders it; whenever it makes an attack roll, an ability check, or a saving throw inside the temple, it must roll a d4 and subtract the number rolled from the d20 roll. In addition, the sensors created by divination spells can't appear inside the temple, and creatures within can't be targeted by divination spells. Finally, whenever any creature in the temple regains hit points from a spell of 1st level or higher, the creature regains additional hit points equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1 hit point). The temple is made from opaque magical force that extends into the Ethereal Plane, thus blocking ethereal travel into the temple's interior. Nothing can physically pass through the temple's exterior. It can't be dispelled by dispel magic, and antimagic field has no effect on it. A disintegrate spell destroys the temple instantly. Casting this spell on the same spot every day for a year makes this effect permanent.

controlOptions.name: Temple Creation
controlOptions.effect: A temple shimmers into existence on visible ground and must fit within an unoccupied cube up to 120 feet on each side.

controlOptions.name: Dedicated By Holy Symbol
controlOptions.effect: The temple is dedicated to the god, pantheon, or philosophy represented by the holy symbol used in casting.

controlOptions.name: Appearance Choices
controlOptions.effect: You make all decisions about the temple's appearance.

controlOptions.name: Door And Windows
controlOptions.effect: The temple has floor, walls, roof, one door, and as many windows as you wish.

controlOptions.name: Designated Door Control
controlOptions.effect: Only you and creatures you designate when casting can open or close the door.

controlOptions.name: Interior Idol Or Altar
controlOptions.effect: The interior is an open space with an idol or altar at one end.

controlOptions.name: Illumination Choice
controlOptions.effect: You decide whether the temple is illuminated, and whether that illumination is bright light or dim light.

controlOptions.name: Incense And Mild Temperature
controlOptions.effect: Burning incense fills the air within, and the temperature is mild.

controlOptions.name: Opposed Creature Types
controlOptions.effect: Choose one or more opposed types from celestials, elementals, fey, fiends, or undead.

controlOptions.name: Opposed Creature Entry Save
controlOptions.effect: A chosen-type creature attempting to enter must make a Charisma save; on a failed save it cannot enter for 24 hours.

controlOptions.name: Opposed Creature D4 Penalty
controlOptions.effect: Chosen-type creatures that can enter subtract 1d4 from attack rolls, ability checks, and saving throws made inside the temple.

controlOptions.name: Divination Sensor Block
controlOptions.effect: Sensors created by divination spells cannot appear inside the temple.

controlOptions.name: Divination Targeting Block
controlOptions.effect: Creatures within cannot be targeted by Divination spells.

controlOptions.name: Healing Bonus
controlOptions.effect: Whenever a creature in the temple regains hit points from a spell of 1st level or higher, it regains additional hit points equal to your Wisdom modifier, minimum 1.

controlOptions.name: Opaque Magical Force
controlOptions.effect: The temple is made from opaque magical force.

controlOptions.name: Ethereal Travel Block
controlOptions.effect: The magical force extends into the Ethereal Plane and blocks ethereal travel into the temple interior.

controlOptions.name: Physical Passage Block
controlOptions.effect: Nothing can physically pass through the temple exterior.

controlOptions.name: Dispel Magic Immune
controlOptions.effect: The temple cannot be dispelled by Dispel Magic.

controlOptions.name: Antimagic Field Immune
controlOptions.effect: Antimagic Field has no effect on the temple.

controlOptions.name: Disintegrate Or Permanent
controlOptions.effect: A Disintegrate spell destroys the temple instantly; casting this spell on the same spot every day for a year makes the effect permanent.

conditionalEndings.description: A Disintegrate spell destroys the temple instantly.

durationProgression.notes: Casting Temple of the Gods on the same spot every day for a year makes the effect permanent.

tensers-floating-disk.name: Tenser's Floating Disk

measuredDetails.label: Follow Distance
measuredDetails.notes: The disk follows so it remains within this distance of you.

measuredDetails.label: Elevation Change Limit
measuredDetails.notes: The disk cannot cross a larger elevation change.

measuredDetails.label: Pit Width Limit
measuredDetails.notes: The disk cannot cross a pit this deep or wide.

measuredDetails.label: Break Distance
measuredDetails.notes: The spell ends if the disk gets farther away than this.

summon.objectDescription: A 3-foot-wide floating disk of force that carries up to 500 lbs, follows within 20 ft, hovers 3 ft above ground, cannot ascend more than 10 ft vertically.

conditionalEndings.description: The spell ends if more than 500 pounds is placed on the disk, and everything on the disk falls to the ground.
The spell ends if the caster moves more than 100 feet from the disk.

tensers-floating-disk.description: This spell creates a circular, horizontal plane of force, 3 feet in diameter and 1 inch thick, that floats 3 feet above the ground in an unoccupied space of your choice that you can see within range. The disk remains for the duration and can hold up to 500 pounds. If more weight is placed on it, the spell ends, and everything on the disk falls to the ground. The disk is immobile while you are within 20 feet of it. If you move more than 20 feet away from it, the disk follows you so that it remains within 20 feet of you. It can move across uneven terrain, up or down stairs, slopes and the like, but it can't cross an elevation change of 10 feet or more. For example, the disk can't move across a 10-foot-deep pit, nor could it leave such a pit if it was created at the bottom. If you move more than 100 feet from the disk (typically because it can't move around an obstacle to follow you), the spell ends.

tensers-transformation.name: Tenser's Transformation

components.materialDescription: a few hairs from a bull

effects.description: The caster gains 50 temporary Hit Points. Any remaining temporary Hit Points from this spell are lost when the spell ends.
When the caster hits a target with a weapon attack, the target takes an extra 2d12 Force damage.
Immediately after the spell ends, the caster must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution save or suffer one level of Exhaustion.
You endow yourself with endurance and martial prowess fueled by magic. Until the spell ends, you can't cast spells, and you gain the following benefits:

controlOptions.name: Cannot Cast Spells
controlOptions.effect: Until the spell ends, the caster cannot cast spells.

controlOptions.name: Simple And Martial Weapon Advantage
controlOptions.effect: The caster has Advantage on attack rolls made with simple and martial weapons.

controlOptions.name: Armor Shield Weapon Proficiency
controlOptions.effect: The caster has proficiency with all armor, shields, simple weapons, and martial weapons.

controlOptions.name: Strength Constitution Save Proficiency
controlOptions.effect: The caster has proficiency in Strength and Constitution saving throws.

controlOptions.name: Extra Attack
controlOptions.effect: The caster can attack twice when taking the Attack action, unless another feature already grants extra attacks.

controlOptions.name: Temporary Hit Points Lost
controlOptions.effect: Any remaining temporary Hit Points from the spell are lost when the spell ends.

controlOptions.name: End Save Exhaustion
controlOptions.effect: Immediately after the spell ends, the caster must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution save or suffer one level of Exhaustion.

tensers-transformation.description: You endow yourself with endurance and martial prowess fueled by magic. Until the spell ends, you can't cast spells, and you gain the following benefits: You gain 50 temporary hit points. If any of these remain when the spell ends, they are lost. You have advantage on attack rolls that you make with simple and martial weapons. When you hit a target with a weapon attack, that target takes an extra 2d12 force damage. You have proficiency with all armor, shields, simple weapons, and martial weapons. You have proficiency in Strength and Constitution saving throws. You can attack twice, instead of once, when you take the Attack action on your turn. You ignore this benefit if you already have a feature, like Extra Attack, that gives you extra attacks. Immediately after the spell ends, you must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or suffer one level of exhaustion.

thaumaturgy.name: Thaumaturgy

options.label: Altered Eyes
options.summary: Alter the appearance of your eyes for 1 minute.

options.label: Booming Voice
options.summary: Make your voice boom up to three times as loud for 1 minute and gain Advantage on Charisma (Intimidation) checks.

options.label: Fire Play
options.summary: Cause flames to flicker, brighten, dim, or change color for 1 minute.

options.label: Invisible Hand
options.summary: Instantly cause an unlocked door or window to fly open or slam shut.

options.label: Phantom Sound
options.summary: Create an instantaneous sound from a chosen point within range.

options.label: Tremors
options.summary: Cause harmless tremors in the ground for 1 minute.

modeChoice.notes: Up to three 1-minute Thaumaturgy effects can be active at a time.

effects.description: Create one minor sign (voice boom, flame change, tremor, door/shutter movement, eye color change, harmless omen) lasting up to 1 minute.

controlOptions.name: Altered Eyes
controlOptions.effect: Alter the appearance of your eyes for 1 minute.

controlOptions.name: Booming Voice
controlOptions.effect: Your voice booms up to three times as loud as normal for 1 minute.
controlOptions.details: For the duration, you have Advantage on Charisma (Intimidation) checks.

controlOptions.name: Fire Play
controlOptions.effect: Cause flames to flicker, brighten, dim, or change color for 1 minute.

controlOptions.name: Invisible Hand
controlOptions.effect: Instantly cause an unlocked door or window to fly open or slam shut.

controlOptions.name: Phantom Sound
controlOptions.effect: Create an instantaneous sound that originates from a point of your choice within range.

controlOptions.name: Tremors
controlOptions.effect: Cause harmless tremors in the ground for 1 minute.

thaumaturgy.description: You manifest a minor wonder within range. You create one of the effects below within range. If you cast this spell multiple times, you can have up to three of its 1-minute effects active at a time. Altered Eyes. You alter the appearance of your eyes for 1 minute. Booming Voice. Your voice booms up to three times as loud as normal for 1 minute. For the duration, you have Advantage on Charisma (Intimidation) checks. Fire Play. You cause flames to flicker, brighten, dim, or change color for 1 minute. Invisible Hand. You instantaneously cause an unlocked door or window to fly open or slam shut. Phantom Sound. You create an instantaneous sound that originates from a point of your choice within range, such as a rumble of thunder, the cry of a raven, or ominous whispers. Tremors. You cause harmless tremors in the ground for 1 minute.

thorn-whip.name: Thorn Whip

components.materialDescription: the stem of a plant with thorns

scaling.customFormula: 1d6 at 1, 2d6 at 5, 3d6 at 11, 4d6 at 17

thorn-whip.description: You create a vine-like whip covered in thorns that lashes out at your command toward a creature in range. Make a melee spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 1d6 Piercing damage, and if it is Large or smaller, you can pull it up to 10 feet closer to you.
thorn-whip.higherLevels: This spell's damage increases by 1d6 when you reach 5th level (2d6), 11th level (3d6), and 17th level (4d6).

thunder-step.name: Thunder Step

soundEmission.description: The thunder caused by disappearing can be heard up to 300 feet from the origin space.

thunder-step.description: You teleport yourself to an unoccupied space you can see within range. Immediately after you disappear, a thunderous boom sounds, and each creature within 10 feet of the space you left must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 3d10 thunder damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The thunder can be heard from up to 300 feet away. You can bring along objects as long as their weight doesn't exceed what you can carry. You can also teleport one willing creature of your size or smaller who is carrying gear up to its carrying capacity. The creature must be within 5 feet of you when you cast this spell, and there must be an unoccupied space within 5 feet of your destination space for the creature to appear in; otherwise, the creature is left behind.
thunder-step.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d10 for each slot level above 3rd.

thunderclap.name: Thunderclap

soundEmission.description: The thunderous sound can be heard up to 100 feet away when the spell is cast.

thunderclap.description: Each creature in a 5-foot Emanation originating from you must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take 1d6 Thunder damage. The spell's thunderous sound can be heard up to 100 feet away.
thunderclap.higherLevels: The spell's damage increases by 1d6 when you reach 5th level (2d6), 11th level (3d6), and 17th level (4d6).

thunderous-smite.name: Thunderous Smite

soundEmission.description: The empowered strike rings with thunder audible within 300 feet of the caster.

thunderous-smite.description: Your strike rings with thunder that is audible within 300 feet of you, and the target takes an extra 2d6 Thunder damage from the attack. Additionally, if the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed 10 feet away from you and have the Prone condition.
thunderous-smite.higherLevels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 1.

thunderwave.name: Thunderwave

soundEmission.description: A thunderous boom is audible within 300 feet when the wave is released.

effects.description: On a failed save, creature is pushed 10 feet away; unsecured objects wholly in the area are pushed 10 feet. Audible out to 300 feet.

thunderwave.description: You unleash a wave of thunderous energy. Each creature in a 15-foot Cube originating from you makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 2d8 Thunder damage and is pushed 10 feet away from you. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only. In addition, unsecured objects that are entirely within the Cube are pushed 10 feet away from you, and a thunderous boom is audible within 300 feet.
thunderwave.higherLevels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 1.

tidal-wave.name: Tidal Wave
tidal-wave.description: You conjure up a wave of water that crashes down on an area within range. The area can be up to 30 feet long, up to 10 feet wide, and up to 10 feet tall. Each creature in that area must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 4d8 bludgeoning damage and is knocked prone . On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and isn't knocked prone . The water then spreads out across the ground in all directions, extinguishing unprotected flames in its area and within 30 feet of it, and then it vanishes.

time-stop.name: Time Stop

effects.description: You briefly stop the flow of time for everyone but yourself. No time passes for other creatures, while you take 1d4 + 1 turns in a row, during which you can use actions and move as normal. This spell ends if one of the actions you use during this period, or any effects that you create during it, affects a creature other than you or an object being worn or carried by someone other than you. In addition, the spell ends if you move to a place more than 1,000 feet from the location where you cast it.

grantedActions.action: Take 1d4 + 1 consecutive turns while other creatures experience no time passing.

controlOptions.name: Stopped time
controlOptions.effect: No time passes for other creatures while the caster acts.

controlOptions.name: Extra turns
controlOptions.effect: The caster takes 1d4 + 1 turns in a row and can use actions and move normally.

controlOptions.name: Affecting others ends spell
controlOptions.effect: The spell ends if an action or created effect affects another creature or an object worn or carried by someone else.

controlOptions.name: Distance ending
controlOptions.effect: The spell ends if the caster moves more than 1,000 feet from the casting location.

time-stop.description: You briefly stop the flow of time for everyone but yourself. No time passes for other creatures, while you take 1d4 + 1 turns in a row, during which you can use actions and move as normal. This spell ends if one of the actions you use during this period, or any effects that you create during it, affects a creature other than you or an object being worn or carried by someone other than you. In addition, the spell ends if you move to a place more than 1,000 feet from the location where you cast it.

tiny-servant.name: Tiny Servant

effects.description: You touch one Tiny, nonmagical object that isn't attached to another object or a surface and isn't being carried by another creature. The target animates and sprouts little arms and legs, becoming a creature under your control until the spell ends or the creature drops to 0 hit points. See Tiny Servant for its statistics. As a bonus action, you can mentally command the creature if it is within 120 feet of you. (If you control multiple creatures with this spell, you can command any or all of them at the same time, issuing the same command to each one.) You decide what action the creature will take and where it will move during its next turn, or you can issue a simple, general command, such as to fetch a key, stand watch, or stack some books. If you issue no commands, the servant does nothing other than defend itself against hostile creatures. Once given an order, the servant continues to follow that order until its task is complete. When the creature drops to 0 hit points, it reverts to its original form, and any remaining damage carries over to that form. At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, you can animate two additional objects for each slot level above 3rd.

conditionalEndings.description: The animated servant form ends when the creature drops to 0 Hit Points, reverting the target to its original object form with any remaining damage carrying over.

tiny-servant.description: You touch one Tiny, nonmagical object that isn't attached to another object or a surface and isn't being carried by another creature. The target animates and sprouts little arms and legs, becoming a creature under your control until the spell ends or the creature drops to 0 hit points. See Tiny Servant for its statistics. As a bonus action, you can mentally command the creature if it is within 120 feet of you. (If you control multiple creatures with this spell, you can command any or all of them at the same time, issuing the same command to each one.) You decide what action the creature will take and where it will move during its next turn, or you can issue a simple, general command, such as to fetch a key, stand watch, or stack some books. If you issue no commands, the servant does nothing other than defend itself against hostile creatures. Once given an order, the servant continues to follow that order until its task is complete. When the creature drops to 0 hit points, it reverts to its original form, and any remaining damage carries over to that form.
tiny-servant.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, you can animate two additional objects for each slot level above 3rd.

toll-the-dead.name: Toll the Dead

scaling.customFormula: 1d8 (or 1d12 if damaged) at level 1; increases by 1 die at levels 5, 11, and 17

soundEmission.description: A single dolorous bell chime is audible within 10 feet of the target when the spell is cast.

toll-the-dead.description: You point at one creature you can see within range, and the single chime of a dolorous bell is audible within 10 feet of the target. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or take 1d8 Necrotic damage. If the target is missing any of its Hit Points, it instead takes 1d12 Necrotic damage.
toll-the-dead.higherLevels: The spell's damage increases by one die when you reach 5th level (2d8 or 2d12), 11th level (3d8 or 3d12), and 17th level (4d8 or 4d12).

tongues.name: Tongues

effects.description: This spell grants the creature you touch the ability to understand any spoken or signed language that it hears or sees. Moreover, when the target communicates by speaking or signing, any creature that knows at least one language can understand it if that creature can hear the speech or see the signing.

tongues.description: This spell grants the creature you touch the ability to understand any spoken or signed language that it hears or sees. Moreover, when the target communicates by speaking or signing, any creature that knows at least one language can understand it if that creature can hear the speech or see the signing.

transmute-rock.name: Transmute Rock
transmute-rock.description: You choose an area of stone or mud that you can see that fits within a 40-foot cube and is within range, and choose one of the following effects. Transmute Rock to Mud. Nonmagical rock of any sort in the area becomes an equal volume of thick, flowing mud that remains for the spell's duration. The ground in the spell's area becomes muddy enough that creatures can sink into it. Each foot that a creature moves through the mud costs 4 feet of movement, and any creature on the ground when you cast the spell must make a Strength saving throw. A creature must also make the saving throw when it moves into the area for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there. On a failed save, a creature sinks into the mud and is restrained, though it can use an action to end the restrained condition on itself by pulling itself free of the mud. If you cast the spell on a ceiling, the mud falls. Any creature under the mud when it falls must make a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes 4d8 bludgeoning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Transmute Mud to Rock. Nonmagical mud or quicksand in the area no more than 10 feet deep transforms into soft stone for the spell's duration. Any creature in the mud when it transforms must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a successful save, a creature is shunted safely to the surface in an unoccupied space. On a failed save, a creature becomes restrained by the rock. A restrained creature, or another creature within reach, can use an action to try to break the rock by succeeding on a DC 20 Strength check or by dealing damage to it. The rock has AC 15 and 25 hit points, and it is immune to poison and psychic damage.

transport-via-plants.name: Transport via Plants

effects.description: For the duration, any creature can use 5 feet of movement to step into the target plant and exit from the destination plant on the same plane.

This spell creates a magical link between a Large or larger inanimate plant within range and another plant, at any distance, on the same plane of existence. You must have seen or touched the destination plant at least once before. For the duration, any creature can step into the target plant and exit from the destination plant by using 5 feet of movement.

controlOptions.name: Large Inanimate Plant Link
controlOptions.effect: The spell links a Large or larger inanimate plant within range to another plant.

controlOptions.name: Seen Or Touched Destination Plant
controlOptions.effect: The caster must have seen or touched the destination plant at least once before.

controlOptions.name: Same Plane Destination
controlOptions.effect: The destination plant must be on the same plane of existence.

controlOptions.name: Any Creature Can Use
controlOptions.effect: For the duration, any creature can step into the target plant and exit from the destination plant.

controlOptions.name: Uses 5 Feet Movement
controlOptions.effect: Using the plant link requires 5 feet of movement.

transport-via-plants.description: This spell creates a magical link between a Large or larger inanimate plant within range and another plant, at any distance, on the same plane of existence. You must have seen or touched the destination plant at least once before. For the duration, any creature can step into the target plant and exit from the destination plant by using 5 feet of movement.

tree-stride.name: Tree Stride

effects.description: You gain the ability to enter a tree and move from inside it to inside another tree of the same kind within 500 feet. Both trees must be living and at least the same size as you. You must use 5 feet of movement to enter a tree. You instantly know the location of all other trees of the same kind within 500 feet and, as part of the move used to enter the tree, can either pass into one of those trees or step out of the tree you're in. You appear in a spot of your choice within 5 feet of the destination tree, using another 5 feet of movement. If you have no movement left, you appear within 5 feet of the tree you entered. You can use this transportation ability only once on each of your turns. You must end each turn outside a tree.

recurringMechanics.notes: The caster must end each turn outside a tree while Tree Stride is active.

effects.description: Once on each turn, the caster can enter a living tree and teleport to another living tree of the same kind within 500 feet, appearing within 5 feet of the destination tree.

tree-stride.description: You gain the ability to enter a tree and move from inside it to inside another tree of the same kind within 500 feet. Both trees must be living and at least the same size as you. You must use 5 feet of movement to enter a tree. You instantly know the location of all other trees of the same kind within 500 feet and, as part of the move used to enter the tree, can either pass into one of those trees or step out of the tree you're in. You appear in a spot of your choice within 5 feet of the destination tree, using another 5 feet of movement. If you have no movement left, you appear within 5 feet of the tree you entered. You can use this transportation ability only once on each of your turns. You must end each turn outside a tree.

true-polymorph.name: True Polymorph

components.materialDescription: a drop of mercury, a dollop of gum arabic, and a wisp of smoke

effects.description: Choose one creature or nonmagical object that you can see within range. The creature shape-shifts into a different creature or a nonmagical object, or the object shape-shifts into a creature (the object must be neither worn nor carried). The transformation lasts for the duration or until the target dies or is destroyed, but if you maintain Concentration on this spell for the full duration, the spell lasts until dispelled. An unwilling creature can make a Wisdom saving throw, and if it succeeds, it isn't affected by this spell. Creature into Creature. If you turn a creature into another kind of creature, the new form can be any kind you choose that has a Challenge Rating equal to or less than the target's Challenge Rating or level. The target's game statistics are replaced by the stat block of the new form, but it retains its Hit Points, Hit Point Dice, alignment, and personality. The target gains a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to the Hit Points of the new form. These Temporary Hit Points vanish if any remain when the spell ends. The target is limited in the actions it can perform by the anatomy of its new form, and it can't speak or cast spells. The target's gear melds into the new form. The creature can't use or otherwise benefit from any of that equipment. Object into Creature. You can turn an object into any kind of creature, as long as the creature's size is no larger than the object's size and the creature has a Challenge Rating of 9 or lower. The creature is Friendly to you and your allies. In combat, it takes its turns immediately after yours, and it obeys your commands. If the spell lasts more than an hour, you no longer control the creature. It might remain Friendly to you, depending on how you have treated it. Creature into Object. If you turn a creature into an object, it transforms along with whatever it is wearing and carrying into that form, as long as the object's size is no larger than the creature's size. The creature's statistics become those of the object, and the creature has no memory of time spent in this form after the spell ends and it returns to normal.

controlOptions.name: Creature into creature
controlOptions.effect: A creature can become another creature with Challenge Rating no higher than the target CR or level.

controlOptions.name: Creature into object
controlOptions.effect: A creature and its worn/carried gear can become an object no larger than the creature.

controlOptions.name: Object into creature
controlOptions.effect: A nonmagical object that is neither worn nor carried can become a creature no larger than the object and CR 9 or lower.

controlOptions.name: Unwilling save
controlOptions.effect: An unwilling creature makes a Wisdom save; success means it is unaffected.

controlOptions.name: Permanent if sustained
controlOptions.effect: If concentration is maintained for the full hour, the spell lasts until dispelled.

controlOptions.name: Creature stat replacement
controlOptions.effect: Creature-into-creature replaces statistics but retains Hit Points, Hit Point Dice, alignment, and personality.

controlOptions.name: Temporary hit points
controlOptions.effect: Creature-into-creature grants temporary hit points equal to the new form Hit Points, which vanish when the spell ends.

controlOptions.name: Action and speech limits
controlOptions.effect: The new creature form limits actions by anatomy and cannot speak or cast spells.

controlOptions.name: Gear meld
controlOptions.effect: The target gear melds into the new form and cannot be used or benefit the creature.

controlOptions.name: Object creature control
controlOptions.effect: An object turned creature is Friendly, acts after the caster, and obeys commands while controlled.

controlOptions.name: Control after one hour
controlOptions.effect: If the spell lasts more than an hour, the caster no longer controls the creature, though it might remain Friendly.

controlOptions.name: No memory object form
controlOptions.effect: A creature turned object has no memory of time spent in that form after returning to normal.

effects.description: Creature-into-creature grants temporary hit points equal to the new form Hit Points.

aiContext.prompt: Ask for the transformation mode, target, unwilling save state, chosen form/object, eligibility, and whether concentration lasted the full hour.

true-resurrection.name: True Resurrection

components.materialDescription: diamonds worth 25,000+ GP, which the spell consumes

effects.description: The dead creature is revived with all Hit Points.

You touch a creature that has been dead for no longer than 200 years and that died for any reason except old age. The creature is revived with all its Hit Points. This spell closes all wounds, neutralizes any poison, cures all magical contagions, and lifts any curses affecting the creature when it died. The spell replaces damaged or missing organs and limbs. If the creature was Undead, it is restored to its non-Undead form. The spell can provide a new body if the original no longer exists, in which case you must speak the creature's name. The creature then appears in an unoccupied space you choose within 10 feet of you.

controlOptions.name: Death eligibility
controlOptions.effect: The target must have been dead no longer than 200 years and must not have died of old age.

controlOptions.name: Full revival
controlOptions.effect: The creature is revived with all Hit Points.

controlOptions.name: Condition and curse restoration
controlOptions.effect: The spell closes wounds, neutralizes poison, cures magical contagions, and lifts curses present when the creature died.

controlOptions.name: Body restoration
controlOptions.effect: The spell replaces damaged or missing organs and limbs and restores an Undead creature to non-Undead form.

controlOptions.name: New body by name
controlOptions.effect: If the original body no longer exists, the spell can provide a new body after the caster speaks the creature name.

controlOptions.name: New body placement
controlOptions.effect: With a new body, the creature appears in an unoccupied space chosen within 10 feet of the caster.

effects.description: If a new body is provided, the creature appears in an unoccupied space chosen within 10 feet of the caster.

aiContext.prompt: Confirm the creature death was within 200 years, not old age, whether the original body exists, and if not, ask for the spoken name and chosen unoccupied space within 10 feet.

true-resurrection.description: You touch a creature that has been dead for no longer than 200 years and that died for any reason except old age. The creature is revived with all its Hit Points. The creature then appears in an unoccupied space you choose within 10 feet of you.

true-seeing.name: True Seeing

components.materialDescription: mushroom powder worth 25+ GP, which the spell consumes

effects.description: For the duration, the willing creature the caster touches has Truesight with a range of 120 feet.

controlOptions.name: Truesight 120 Feet
controlOptions.effect: The willing touched creature has Truesight with a range of 120 feet for the duration.

true-seeing.description: For the duration, the willing creature you touch has Truesight with a range of 120 feet.

true-strike.name: True Strike

components.materialDescription: a weapon with which you have proficiency and that is worth 1+ CP

effects.description: Make one weapon attack using your spellcasting ability. Damage can be Radiant or normal type.

weaponRequirement.notes: The spell component is a weapon with which the caster has proficiency and that is worth at least 1 CP.

grantedAttack.notes: The spell makes one attack with the weapon used in the spell's casting.

damageTypeChoice.notes: If the attack deals damage, the caster chooses Radiant damage or the weapon's normal damage type.

attackAugments.notes: The 0d6 rider marks the pre-upgrade baseline; character-level scaling below supplies the later Radiant dice.

scaling.customFormula: Extra 1d6 Radiant damage at level 5, 2d6 at 11, 3d6 at 17

true-strike.description: Guided by a flash of magical insight, you make one attack with the weapon used in the spell's casting. The attack uses your spellcasting ability for the attack and damage rolls instead of using Strength or Dexterity. If the attack deals damage, it can be Radiant damage or the weapon's normal damage type (your choice).
true-strike.higherLevels: Whether you deal Radiant damage or the weapon's normal damage type, the attack deals extra Radiant damage when you reach levels 5 (1d6), 11 (2d6), and 17 (3d6).

tsunami.name: Tsunami

sizes: Huge or smaller for ongoing wall-movement damage

effects.description: A wall of water springs into existence at a point you choose within range. You can make the wall up to 300 feet long, 300 feet high, and 50 feet thick. The wall lasts for the duration. When the wall appears, each creature in its area makes a Strength saving throw, taking 6d10 Bludgeoning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. At the start of each of your turns after the wall appears, the wall, along with any creatures in it, moves 50 feet away from you. Any Huge or smaller creature inside the wall or whose space the wall enters when it moves must succeed on a Strength saving throw or take 5d10 Bludgeoning damage. A creature can take this damage only once per round. At the end of the turn, the wall's height is reduced by 50 feet, and the damage the wall deals on later rounds is reduced by 1d10. When the wall reaches 0 feet in height, the spell ends. A creature caught in the wall can move by swimming. Because of the wave's force, though, the creature must succeed on a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC to move at all. If it fails the check, it can't move. A creature that moves out of the wall falls to the ground.

controlOptions.name: Water wall dimensions
controlOptions.effect: The caster can make the water wall up to 300 feet long, 300 feet high, and 50 feet thick.

controlOptions.name: Initial impact
controlOptions.effect: When the wall appears, each creature in the area makes a Strength save against 6d10 Bludgeoning damage.

controlOptions.name: Moving wall damage
controlOptions.effect: A Huge or smaller creature inside the wall or whose space the wall enters saves against 5d10 Bludgeoning damage, only once per round.

controlOptions.name: Round decay
controlOptions.effect: At the end of each turn, wall height is reduced by 50 feet and later-round damage is reduced by 1d10.

controlOptions.name: Swim restriction
controlOptions.effect: A creature caught in the wall can move by swimming only if it succeeds on a Strength (Athletics) check against the spell save DC.

controlOptions.name: Fall out
controlOptions.effect: A creature that moves out of the wall falls to the ground.

controlOptions.name: Height ending
controlOptions.effect: When the wall reaches 0 feet in height, the spell ends.

effects.description: When the wall appears, each creature in its area makes a Strength save against 6d10 Bludgeoning damage.
At the start of each caster turn after the wall appears, Huge or smaller creatures inside the wall or whose space the wall enters save against ongoing Bludgeoning damage.
At the start of each caster turn after the wall appears, the wall and any creatures in it move 50 feet away from the caster.

forcedMovement.maxDistance: 50 feet at the start of each caster turn after the wall appears
